description: Research ledger for Lean PS: the shared commuting/equivariance schema and its two instances (Kripke-Joyal forcing persistence; Lorentz-force covariance). Statuses follow the forcing paper's discipline: P proved relative to stated hypotheses, D definitional architecture, S specification/conformance contract, M modeling postulate, C measurable conjecture, O open. Direct deps/consumes are stored here; the transitive M/C/O debt closure is computed mechanically by tools/leanps_audit.py, which also regenerates docs/18_LEAN_PS_LEDGER.md. The two instances are NOT identified: the forcing instance is relational, partial, Prop-valued preservation; the physics instance is total, group-indexed, on-the-nose equality. Same interface, different semantics.

M-Pers.where: forcing paper status ledger; Lean: Persistent typeclass; probed by kernel/sweep.py E7

hypotheses: collapsed and seed states are distinct
internal repair and boundary intervention fix the collapsed state
collapse inversion maps collapsed state to seed
all escaping mechanisms are inversion for the uniqueness conclusion
entries.falsifier: Internal or boundary mechanisms escaping despite their fixed-state laws, inversion failing to escape despite reaching a distinct seed, or base laws alone excluding the exhibited second escape.

entries.statement: Book7NoncontextualHilbert.lean: parallelogram geometry and guarded noncontextuality at p=2.
hypotheses: frame-independent coherence iff the effective geometry has the parallelogram property
the relevant Lp geometry has the parallelogram property iff p=2
entries.falsifier: Euclidean squared norm violating the parallelogram identity, L1 satisfying it on the exhibited basis pair, or either explicit bridge failing to transport noncontextuality to p=2.

entries.statement: AppendixCurvatureFlows.lean: complete bounded-flow ambient space and closed observer Lipschitz bounds.
hypotheses: complete normed target for ambient bounded continuous flows
pointwise convergence of the flow sequence
one common Lipschitz constant across the sequence
entries.falsifier: A Cauchy bounded-continuous flow sequence lacking a limit in its complete ambient space, a common distance bound failing in the pointwise limit, or pointwise convergence alone forcing the zero-Lipschitz bound in the exhibited identity sequence.

entries.statement: Book6ThermodynamicMAP.lean: oriented mean-balance residual for thermodynamic-MAP duality.
hypotheses: the mean drift decomposes constitutively into inverse-temperature projected force plus reflective deviation
entries.falsifier: The displayed balance failing residual-zero equivalence, either algebraic rearrangement changing sign, or qualitative flags alone forcing the equation in the exhibited countermodel.

entries.statement: Book7LpRegression.lean: observer-relative free-energy minimization as finite Lp regression along a reflective orbit.
hypotheses: on the feasible basin, or at minimum along the witnessed reflective orbit, free energy is a positive affine rescaling of the selected finite Lp loss
entries.falsifier: A positive affine representation reversing objective order; a represented reflective descent step increasing Lp loss; or bounded-below descent forcing a representation in the exhibited equal-loss, unequal-energy countermodel.

entries.statement: Book7BornCollapse.lean: contextuality-defect collapse to the unique Hilbert cross-section, with guarded Born readout.
hypotheses: zero contextuality defect iff exponent p=2 iff the unique Hilbert frame
reflective fixed point iff zero contextuality defect
the convergent orbit has continuous defect tending to zero
the Born readout additionally requires an explicit Gleason-style uniqueness bridge
entries.falsifier: A non-Hilbert frame with zero defect, a second reflective fixed point, a convergent zero-defect orbit limiting away from the Hilbert frame, or Hilbert collapse alone selecting a unique readout despite the exhibited countermodel.

entries.statement: Book8FramingEquivalence.lean: universal product-span criterion and guarded curvature-entanglement equivalence.
hypotheses: symbolic curvature vanishes iff the observer projection residual vanishes
projection residual vanishes iff some locally admissible subsystem pair places the observed difference in its product span
entries.falsifier: A separable state excluded from every admissible product span, either bridge failing to compose, or a bare curvature scalar forcing entanglement in the exhibited countermodel.

entries.statement: Book8SRConvergence.lean: coercive Lyapunov convergence of an SR orbit to a step-invariant set.
hypotheses: the invariant set is nonempty and closed under the SR step
the nonnegative free-energy gap decreases and tends to zero along the orbit
distance to the invariant set is bounded by a nonnegative constant times the free-energy gap
entries.falsifier: A represented SR step increasing free energy, the invariant set failing step closure, the coercively controlled distance failing to tend to zero, or monotone energy alone forcing convergence in the constant-distance countermodel.

entries.statement: Book9CurvatureScarring.lean: oriented betrayal scar and resource-guarded recovery into revised reciprocity.
hypotheses: betrayal drift is nonnegative in the selected curvature orientation and exactly produces the curvature displacement
grace plus drift-within-capacity and repair-cost-within-energy is the explicit law for revised reciprocity
entries.falsifier: The oriented scar disagreeing with betrayal drift, absolute magnitude changing its nonnegative value, grace and adequate resources failing the stated recovery law, recovery requiring scar erasure, or resources alone forcing grace.

entries.statement: Book9EthicalIntervention.lean: typed intervention and restraint signals with conflict review and independent execution authority.
hypotheses: the source-listed viability, covenant, and consent conditions are represented as disjunctive justification signals
the source-listed reflective capacity, self-healing, and interface-risk conditions are represented as disjunctive restraint signals
execution requires authority independently of a technical recommendation
entries.falsifier: A case escaping the four-way classification, simultaneous strict intervention and non-intervention recommendations, conflict silently selecting intervention, or a recommendation alone proving authority.

entries.statement: AppendixTitansArrow.lean: test-time memorization inherits the Appendix C arrow of time only through an explicit MemoryAct witness.
hypotheses: the external test-time process supplies a history order strictly increased by every memory step
every represented memory step has strictly positive cost
full process state includes history rather than only the visible model coordinate
entries.falsifier: A witnessed memory act preserving history, a positive iterate returning to initial history, visible-coordinate return implying full-state return, or every bare test-time update being irreversible despite the reversible Bool witness.
